sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.91 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is said to be in$G$if there is a subgroup$B G$such that$G=N_{G}(H)B$and$H$permutes with every subgroup of~$B$. a) Is there a finite group$G$with subgroups$A B G$such that$A$is propermutable in$G$but$A$is not propermutable in~$B$? b) Let$P$be a non-abelian Sylow 2-subgroup of a finite group$G$with$|P|=2^{n}$. Suppose that there is an integer$k$such that$1 < k < n$and every...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
